changelog:
- New board variant: 9M2IBR_ESP32_E22-400M30S_30dBm_096OLED - same Ebyte E22-400M30S (30dBm/400MHz) module as the existing 1.3" variant, but wired to a 0.96" OLED (SSD1306 controller) instead of the usual 1.3" panel (SH1106 controller). Same resolution/I2C wiring, so no other display code changes.
- Web config: exposed LoRa TX power (the SX1268 chip's own register value, -9..22dBm) as an editable field per frequency profile, instead of being hardcoded to 20 with no UI - the saved value is clamped server-side to what the built variant's radio.setOutputPower() can actually accept, so an out-of-range value can't hang the firmware at boot.
- Web flasher: added a "Firmware update only" install mode alongside the existing full factory install - writes just the app firmware and leaves the existing SPIFFS config (callsign, WiFi, beacons, etc.) untouched, so routine updates no longer require reconfiguring the tracker from scratch. Also added a board variant reference table to the flasher page, and a favicon for the flasher site (reuses the firmware's existing icon) - it was previously falling back to the browser's default tab icon.

- CPU speed control - the CPU clock was previously hardcoded to 80MHz at boot with no way to change it. Added a persisted setting (80/160/240MHz) selectable from Configuration > Display > CPU Speed on-device or from the Display section of the web config UI, applied immediately and saved to flash.
- Smoother battery reading - on boards without ADC_CTRL, the on-screen battery voltage/percentage was resampled every loop() iteration and visibly flickered from ADC noise on the raw resistor-divider path. The ADC read is now 16x-oversampled, and the displayed value updates at most once a second with exponential smoothing instead of jumping with every sample.
- Web config: fixed the mobile (collapsed) navbar menu, where each tab centered independently on its own text width instead of lining up - tabs are now left-justified with consistent spacing on narrow screens.

- APRS-IS login is now verified before trusting the connection - previously the firmware marked the session connected right after writing the login line without checking the server's response, so an unverified login (bad passcode/callsign) silently dropped every packet with no indication why. It now waits for and checks the server's logresp reply.
- Delayed APRS-IS queue hardening: capped at 20 entries (oldest dropped first) instead of growing unbounded during an outage, and a backlog of overdue entries is now paced at least 2 seconds apart instead of being sent in one burst.
- APRS-IS TCP writes are now checked and retried: a failed/partial write to the delayed queue is kept and retried (up to 3 attempts) instead of silently discarding that beacon. One-shot sends (message acks, iGate RF>IS forwarding) now log a warning on incomplete writes.
- Web config: navbar title now shows just the callsign ("9M2IBR's LoRa Tracker") instead of including the SSID; the Message tab callsign input is narrower, giving the Send-via checkboxes more room.

- Process inbound APRS-IS messages: messages sent to your callsign over APRS-IS are now received, displayed, saved to the on-device store, logged in the web Message tab, and acked back (previously only outbound uplink worked).
- Fixed the time source indicator falsely showing 'G' (GPS) before any time sync had actually happened.

- Web config navbar now shows the tracker's actual active callsign instead of a static label.
- WiFi STA network list: replaced the text remove button with a trash-can icon.
- New "About" tab: callsign, firmware version, LoRa module/power/band, hardware ID, chip info, flash size, free heap, uptime.
- New "Message" tab: send APRS messages via LoRa RF and/or APRS-IS, view/delete the last 10 sent/received messages.
- Various web UI layout and navigation fixes.

- Web config dark mode: Light/Dark/Auto theme switcher, remembered per-browser.
- Fixed a choppy "Last Rx" ticker on the OLED display.
- Messages > APRSThursday menu now exits back to the previous menu after the last item instead of wrapping around.

- First versioned release of this fork: trimmed the board list down to the 4 variants actually in use, rebranded startup/log/web-UI text.
- Fixed a batch of bugs found during review: config-load crashes on corrupt/missing data, a stack buffer overflow from unvalidated received-packet data, an out-of-bounds symbol-array read, ISR-unsafe shared state on button/joystick input, a WiFi STA reconnect loop issue, and several SmartBeacon/menu-navigation/units logic bugs.
